cleanTargets.description: Temporary files created by applications and the current user session
System-wide temporary files; typically cleared on reboot by tmpfiles.d
Temporary files that persist across reboots; safe to clean periodically
Cached thumbnails generated by file managers following the freedesktop spec
Kernel and application crash dump files collected by apport or similar
Persistent systemd journal log files; can grow very large over time
Downloaded .deb packages cached by APT (Debian/Ubuntu); equivalent to apt clean
Downloaded RPM packages and metadata cached by DNF (Fedora/RHEL)
Downloaded packages cached by Pacman (Arch Linux); equivalent to pacman -Sc
Downloaded snap packages no longer needed after installation
Per-snap user cache directories inside the Snap sandbox
Per-app cache directories for installed Flatpak applications
Temporary files from Flatpak repository operations and downloads
Downloaded RPM packages cached by Zypper (openSUSE)
Application core dump files collected by systemd-coredump
Fontconfig font rendering cache; rebuilt automatically on demand
Log files from system installation and initial setup
System-wide fontconfig cache; rebuilt automatically by fc-cache
Cached formatted manual pages; rebuilt on next access
APT package manager history and terminal log files (Debian/Ubuntu)
GNOME Tracker file indexer and search database cache; rebuilt automatically
Legacy GNOME Tracker file indexer cache (pre-Tracker 3); rebuilt automatically
GStreamer multimedia framework plugin registry cache; rebuilt automatically

singleFileTargets.description: stderr output from the previous X11 session
